U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says China will effectively be cut off from Iranian oil: Those ships aren’t going to be allowed out anymore… they’re not going to be able to get their oil. They can get oil. Not Iranian oil.

Ukraine’s Military Intelligence(GUR) says it secretly carried out two rocket launches to near-space/space during the war—one above 100 km (crossing into space) and another reaching 204 km. They also tested a rocket launched from an aircraft at ~8,000 m, which they say is Europe’s first air-launch of its kind. This approach could eventually enable an “airborne launch system” for missiles or even small satellites. Source: Defence Blog

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇨🇳 Reuters: A Chinese tanker sanctioned by the United States has passed through the Strait of Hormuz despite the U.S. blockade, according to shipping data. The vessel, identified as Rich Starry, is reported to have carried around 250,000 barrels of methanol…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇨🇳 Reuters: The U.S.-sanctioned Chinese tanker Rich Starry returned to the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday after briefly exiting the Gulf, failing to break the U.S. blockade. It had been one of at least eight ships crossing the waterway on the first day of the blockade. @wfwitness

Iran secretly acquired a Chinese-built spy satellite (TEE-01B) in late 2024 via the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps aerospace unit. Leaked documents suggest the satellite was then used to monitor key U.S. military bases across the Middle East, producing time-stamped imagery and coordinates. These were used before and after drone and missile strikes to help plan attacks and assess damage. Source: FT

🇷🇺 💬 Russia can make up for the energy resource deficit of China and other interested countries, which arose against the backdrop of the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Lavrov said during his visit to China. Other statements by the head of the Russian Foreign Ministry: ➡️ Putin's visit to China will take place in the first half of 2026. ➡️ Lavrov reported that he discussed the aggravation of the situation in the world due to the actions of the West, the situation in the Middle East, the militarization of the EU, and the crisis in NATO. ➡️ The US and Iran should promote realistic goals in the negotiations, and Russia and China are ready to promote any formats of their external support. ➡️ Moscow will accept any decision by Iran on enriched uranium and is ready to play a role in resolving this issue. ➡️ Relations between Russia and the US are not at a "freezing point", contacts are being conducted, but not all of them are being reported on. ➡️ At the negotiations on Ukraine in Alaska, the discussion focused on recognizing the de jure realities on the ground, and Moscow remains committed to the agreements reached at the summit. ➡️ US sanctions against Russia remain in place, as does the discrimination of Russian energy companies in the market. ➡️ The West is plotting a new military bloc, in which Ukraine is a leading participant. ➡️ Macron is reveling in the idea of creating "stabilization forces" in Ukraine. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

The main contenders include: Right…" ⚡️ 🇵🇪 AP: Peru’s election is facing major voting disruptions after authorities failed to deliver ballots and election materials to multiple polling stations on time, delaying the opening of voting centers across the country and abroad. The issues left thousands of voters unable to cast ballots as scheduled, forcing officials to extend voting into an additional day in affected areas. Election observers said the problems were logistical in nature, with an EU mission stating there was no sufficient evidence to support claims of fraud, despite criticism from some candidates, namely López Aliaga. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇵🇪 Peru voted in its 2026 general election following a turbulent period that saw seven presidents in nine years. A total of 34 candidates are competing in a crowded first round where the top two advance to a June runoff. The main contenders include: Right-wing Keiko Fujimori of Popular Force Far-right Rafael López Aliaga of Renovación Popular Left-wing Roberto Sánchez of Juntos por el Perú Centrist Jorge Nieto of Partido del Buen Gobierno Populist Ricardo Belmont. With votes 82% counted, Fujimori currently leads with about 17%, followed by a tight cluster of López Aliaga, Sánchez, Nieto, and Belmont each around 10–12%, leaving the second runoff spot still undecided as more votes are counted. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 WSJ: U.S. naval forces have intercepted eight oil tankers entering or leaving Iranian ports since the start of the blockade on Monday. Officials say U.S. forces contacted the crews by radio and ordered them to turn back, with all vessels complying and no boardings taking place. The first two interceptions reportedly involved ships departing Iran’s Chabahar port and were directed by a U.S. destroyer, with P-8 maritime patrol aircraft also involved. @wfwitness
